summary: Fix `temper session-start` on a project with a registered custom kind — resolve authored `.temper/kinds` + `.temper/packages` from the harness, not the throwaway scratch surface

per.section: Decision: the session-start gate is advisory, not blocking

edit.description: The one-shot `SessionStart` arm (main.rs:363-388) imports members into a throwaway `scratch_surface()` and calls `gate(&scratch, &harness_path.join(TEMPER_TOML))` (main.rs:380). But `gate` (main.rs:456) derives `packages_dir`/`kinds_dir` from the *workspace* it is handed (the scratch surface, main.rs:473/479), which never carries the project's authored `.temper/kinds/<name>/KIND.md` or `.temper/packages/` — so a registered custom kind dangles as `kind::missing_definition`. Give `gate` a separate authored-root parameter for `kinds_dir`/`packages_dir` and pass it from both call sites: `check` uses the workspace itself (behaviour unchanged), the session-start arm uses the harness's own `.temper/` beside its `temper.toml`.

Regression fixture: a harness that registers a custom kind (its KIND.md + bound package authored under `.temper/`) passes `session-start` with an advisory payload instead of erroring with kind::missing_definition.

tests.asserts: `session-start` over a harness with a registered custom kind resolves the authored KIND.md + package from the harness's `.temper/` and exits zero with the reporter payload, never kind::missing_definition.

acceptance: `temper session-start <harness>` on a project with a registered custom kind resolves the authored KIND.md + package from the harness's own `.temper/` and emits the advisory reporter payload (exit 0), instead of failing with kind::missing_definition; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: Found dogfooding (inbox): the installed SessionStart hook at temper's own root errors every session — its custom `spec` kind's definition/packages live in `.temper/`, not the scratch import surface. High priority. Serialized behind COVERAGE-CUSTOM-KIND (both edit src/main.rs); EMBED-BUILTIN-PACKAGES now chains behind THIS. Fix is the one-shot path only — `check`'s two-step resolution is already correct.

summary: Relicense to dual MIT OR Apache-2.0 — add LICENSE-MIT + LICENSE-APACHE and set the Cargo.toml `license` field, before external contributors

per.section: Decision: the license is MIT OR Apache-2.0, dual, before launch

new.description: The standard Apache-2.0 license text (the patent grant the dual choice buys).

edit.description: Set `license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"` (the SPDX expression the dual grant requires; the API-guidelines / uv / Biome choice), replacing the current `license = "MIT"` (Cargo.toml:6). The `license` field follows the LICENSE change, per the Decision.

acceptance: The repo carries LICENSE-MIT + LICENSE-APACHE and Cargo.toml declares `license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"`; cargo build/fmt/clippy green.
notes: From the offering tranche (specs/55-offering.md). Do the relicense now — the Decision's window is 'while there are no external contributors' (clean trunk confirms it). No bare LICENSE file exists on disk; the only license declaration is Cargo.toml `license = "MIT"` (Cargo.toml:6), flipped to SPDX `MIT OR Apache-2.0` — nothing to retire. Disjoint from all other open entries: owns Cargo.toml `license` + the LICENSE-* files, touches no source/README.

summary: Author AGENTS.md at root — contributor-facing build/test/lint + architecture map, pointing to the AI-authorship policy; CLAUDE.md stays canonical and untouched

per.section: The front door — the README is the landing page

new.description: The 2025-26 agent-readable contributor doc at repo root: build/test/lint commands (cargo build/test, clippy -D warnings, fmt), a short architecture map of `src/` (the contract engine, kinds, surface, drift), and a pointer to the AI-contribution policy in `.github/CONTRIBUTING.md` (shipped by OFFERING-COMMUNITY, 4e87522). Per the RESOLVED (launch-front-door-docs) fork it is a SEPARATE contributor doc — it does NOT touch `.claude/**` or `CLAUDE.md`, and CLAUDE.md is NOT gutted to source it (the ruff/uv 'CLAUDE.md = @AGENTS.md' move is rejected here; CLAUDE.md is the pipeline's operating harness, the recursive-dogfood fixture).

acceptance: AGENTS.md exists at root as a contributor-facing doc (build/test/lint + architecture map + pointer to the AI-authorship policy); CLAUDE.md and `.claude/**` are untouched; cargo green.
notes: Fileable per RESOLVED (launch-front-door-docs). Root doc only, disjoint from every other entry (owns AGENTS.md alone). The chore(harness) boundary holds: this entry must not edit CLAUDE.md or `.claude/**`. CONTRIBUTING.md lives at `.github/CONTRIBUTING.md` (verified on disk), so link there.

summary: Embed the built-in std-lib from temper's own `packages/` PACKAGE.md sources and retire `contracts/*.toml` — the dogfood un-park validating the reconciled machinery

per.section: Decision: a package is project-authorable, not vendor-privileged — and is itself a kind

new.description: Embed the authored package directories (`packages/{skill.anthropic,rule.anthropic}/PACKAGE.md`) into the binary via a build script — auto-detected at package root (no Cargo.toml edit needed if it reads the sources and generates `include!`d code; if `include_dir` is used instead, its dependency is added here). Folded in per the resolved (package-surface-sequencing) fork; keeps the embed off Cargo.toml so it never collides with OFFERING-LICENSE.

edit.description: Built-in names resolve from the embedded PACKAGE.md sources via `Contract::load_package` (contract.rs:586) instead of `include_str!("../contracts/*.toml")` (main.rs:59/65). Shares this file with COVERAGE-CUSTOM-KIND and SESSION-START-CUSTOM-KIND — the blockedBy chain serializes all three.
The plugin ships the embedded PACKAGE.md std-lib verbatim instead of the TOML files (bundle.rs:122/126, 210/216).
Retire the TOML-file contract loader (`Contract::load`) once nothing resolves through it; keep `Contract::load_package`.

tests.asserts: built-ins resolve from the embedded authored sources; the bundled plugin carries them; nothing references contracts/*.toml.

acceptance: One artifact, two provenance roles: the same PACKAGE.md is authored as product source and shipped embedded; `contracts/` is gone; the std-lib resolves (skill→skill.anthropic, rule→rule.anthropic); cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: Un-parked: packages/{skill.anthropic,rule.anthropic}/PACKAGE.md are authored (966147d); loader Contract::load_package exists (contract.rs:586). On disk: contracts/{skill.anthropic,rule}.toml still exist, main.rs:59/65 + bundle.rs:122/126,210/216 include_str! them. Last in the main.rs chain (COVERAGE → SESSION-START → EMBED). rule→rule.anthropic rename lands here (20-surface.md binds rule→rule.anthropic).

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md` imported as one AGENT.md member document, checked by an authored agent package

gate.reason: the requirements/satisfies reframe deprioritizes more built-in kinds; the surface-language chain it waited on (through KIND-AUTHORED-ARTIFACT + GRAPH-CUSTOM-KIND) has shipped, so the blocker is priority alone — revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The Agent IR — the typed model for a Claude Code subagent at `.claude/agents/<name>.md`, mirroring `src/rule.rs`: typed YAML frontmatter on the projection side (`name`, `description`, optional `tools`/`model`), unknown keys preserved, content-faithful body, provenance. Identity is the path (file stem). The surface member is one AGENT.md document (clause-module header) like every kind.

The agent kind's authored built-in package (clauses + guidance) in the one-document medium, embedded with the std-lib (the EMBED-BUILTIN-PACKAGES model — NOT a contracts/agent.toml). Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores; an advisory `max_lines`.
Fixture-driven proof mirroring `tests/rule_contract.rs`: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an insta import round-trip proving idempotence.

edit.description: Add `pub mod agent;` beside the other artifact-kind modules (this entry owns the line).
Extend `Workspace` with `agents` loaded under `<dir>/agents/*` and a `WorkspaceError` arm; update the load tests.
Discover `.claude/agents/*.md`, project each to one `<into>/agents/<name>/AGENT.md` member document, add an `[[agent]]` lock row per agent.
Bind the agent package, wire admissibility + conformance streams and `by_kind` for the kind.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the agent package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` projects agents as one-document members idempotently with `[[agent]]` lock rows; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Deferred by the requirements/satisfies reframe: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction. Shaped to the surface-language forms — one AGENT.md member document, an authored PACKAGE.md, no meta.toml, no contracts/agent.toml. On revival its shared-surface blast radius (lib/check/extract/import/main) serializes against any then-open entry that edits main.rs (COVERAGE, EMBED).

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than blindly clobbering the harness manifest. Scoped/prefixed names per the resolved (project-name) fork.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB: root `package.json` already exists as the *private* flume manifest — reconcile or split, never blindly create a second. Parked until a human sets creds up.
